scenario: Orion Retail's checkout form on Camunda 8.8 prices a shopping cart for 9500 customers per day, and the modeler adds an Expression field with key cartTotal whose Target value sums the cart line prices via a FEEL expression. The team wants cartTotal to recompute every time a quantity or unit-price changes so the displayed total stays in sync with the cart edits in real time.
question: Which Compute on setting does the modeler select for the cartTotal Expression field to meet that recalculation requirement?

options.text: Compute on set to whenever the result changes, so the FEEL Target value for cartTotal is re-evaluated each time an input variable to the expression changes during cart editing in the form
Compute on set to only on form submission, which defers the evaluation of the cartTotal FEEL Target value to the moment the customer clicks the submit button to finalise the checkout
Compute on set to whenever a sibling field is focused, which re-evaluates the cartTotal FEEL Target value as soon as the customer tabs into a related row of the cart in the form
Compute on set to at form load only, which evaluates the cartTotal FEEL Target value once at the time the checkout form is first rendered for the customer and never again afterwards

a.text: Correct. The Compute on property of the Expression form element offers two documented modes: whenever the result changes, or only on form submission. To keep cartTotal in sync with cart edits, the modeler selects the change-driven mode.

b.text: Incorrect. The on-submission mode delays evaluation until the submit step, so the displayed cartTotal would not update interactively as quantities or unit-prices change in the cart during editing.

c.text: Incorrect. The Compute on property documents two modes (result-change and on-submission); a focus-driven mode tied to sibling fields is not part of the documented options for the field.

d.text: Incorrect. The documented options for Compute on are result-change and on-submission; a one-time at-form-load evaluation is not part of the Expression field's documented behaviour for cartTotal.

explanation: The Expression form element computes intermediary data via a FEEL Target value. Its Compute on property has exactly two documented modes: re-evaluate whenever the result changes, or evaluate only on form submission. The interactive cart total requires the change-driven mode.
